description: Duplicate a vector or line segment at a new starting point, preserving its original direction and magnitude.
utility: Copy vector/line to new starting point preserving direction and magnitude

arguments.name: point
arguments.description: New starting point - either a point expression or x, y coordinates

alternates.description: Copy vector to start at a point expression
Copy vector to start at (x, y) coordinates
Copy line segment as vector to new start point

examples.scenario: Copy vector to a new starting point
examples.expression:
V = vector(G, 0, 0, 3, 2)
V2 = placeat(G, V, point(G, 1, 1))

examples.scenario: Build a parallelogram with vector placement
examples.expression:
A = point(G, 0, 0)
B = point(G, 4, 0)
V1 = vector(G, 0, 0, 4, 0)
V2 = vector(G, 0, 0, 1, 3)
V3 = placeat(G, V1, point(G, 1, 3))
V4 = placeat(G, V2, point(G, 4, 0))

examples.scenario: Copy line segment as vector to new position
examples.expression:
L = line(G, 0, 0, 3, 4)
V = placeat(G, L, point(G, 5, 0))
